Name: Ashcombe Shooting Ground
Full Address: Colley Lane, Ashcombe, Devon, EX7 0QD
Description: Ashcombe Shooting Ground, nestled in the picturesque Ashcombe Estate near Dawlish, Devon, is the South West’s premier clay‑shooting destination, with over 30 years of heritage and recent reinvestment under the Sportsman Gun Centre group. The ground boasts an extensive layout featuring high‑tower, sporting and skeet ranges supported by more than 100 automatic traps offering varied presentations from battue to midi targets. Practice shooting is available Wednesday through Saturday (9:30 am-5 pm), and Sunday (10 am–3 pm) with no prior booking required for licence‑holders - just turn up and shoot. The venue also hosts a range of competitions - including Registered English Sporting, Supersporting 100‑target events, and the Browning Sporting Masters - and welcomes novice to advanced shooters alike. Professional one‑on‑one lessons, corporate packages, gun hire, and cartridges are available onsite, along with on‑site catering and a friendly, instructional environment for beginners.

Name: Willow Farm Shooting Ground
Full Address: Willow Farm Bungalow, Reading Street, Tenterden, Kent, TN30 7LA
Description: Willow Farm Shooting Ground, located near Tenterden in the Kent countryside, is a 29‑acre clay‑shooting venue operating for over 30 years. The ground offers practice sessions and lessons in Sporting, Skeet, DTL, and Compact layouts from Wednesday through Saturday, while hosting competitive events - including weekly Sunday Sporting competitions and occasional registered 100‑bird English Sporting shoots - for novices through AAA‑level shooters. Experienced instructors provide coaching and group packages, and the site includes ample parking, refreshments, and facilities to ensure an enjoyable visit.

Name: Purbeck Shooting School
Full Address: Puddletown Road, Wareham, Dorset, BH20 7NG
Description: Purbeck Shooting School, founded by Graham Brown in 1995 and now led by his son Sam, is a top‑rated clay target venue located on about 80 acres of heath and conifer woodland near Wareham in Dorset. Known for its varied terrain and natural contours, the school offers tuition for novices as well as experienced shooters, FITASC and English Sporting layouts, ladies‑only sessions, stag and hen parties, and corporate events

Name: Royal Berkshire Shooting Ground
Full Address: Hook End Lane Pangbourne, Reading RG8 8SD
Description: The Royal Berkshire Shooting Ground - also known today as Purdey at the Royal Berkshire Shooting School - is a premier clay shooting facility set across approximately 100 acres of scenic Downland near Pangbourne, Berkshire, just about an hour’s drive or fast‑train ride from Central London. Established in the early 1990s and acquired by the distinguished London-based gunmaker Purdey in 2018 (with rebranding in 2022), the ground is widely regarded as one of the UK’s finest shooting schools

Name: Owls Lodge Shooting Ground
Full Address: Drayton Road, Barton Stacey, Winchester, Hampshire, SO21 3QS
Description: Owls Lodge Shooting School, founded in 2010 by Olympic double‑trap champion Richard Faulds MBE on the family’s Owl’s Lodge farm in Barton Stacey, Hampshire, offers a premier clay‑pigeon experience across rolling woodland just off the A303 near Winchester. Open for general practice most Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:00 to 18:00, the school features registered CPSA events in disciplines including English Sporting, Sportrap, Down The Line and FITASC, along with tuition for shooters at all levels. Its layout includes 14 or more stands thoughtfully integrated to allow multiple games from a single position, making it both varied and accessible to novices and experienced shooters alike. With expert instruction, well‑maintained grounds and a welcoming clubhouse, Owls Lodge provides both recreational and competitive clay shooting in a scenic rural setting.

Name: Fauxdegla International Clay Shooting Centre
Full Address: Llandegla, Wrexham, LL11 3AL
Description: Fauxdegla International Clay Shooting Centre, located near Wrexham in North Wales, is a Premier Plus CPSA ground offering one of the UK’s top clay shooting experiences. Set on scenic Llandegla Moor, it features 12 sporting stands, Compak, Sportrap, DTL, Skeet, ABT, Olympic Trap, and Universal Trench—all fully covered and many wheelchair accessible.
Practice Notes: Open Wed to Sun 10am - 5pm. Late night on Wed during the summer.

Name: Ferndale Shooting Club
Full Address: CF43 4UF, Ferndale, Rhondda Cynon Taf, Wales, United Kingdom
Description: Ferndale Shooting Club, established in 1984, is a clay pigeon shooting ground nestled at the top of Rhondda Fach Valley, between Ynysybwl and Ferndale, near Llanwonno and the Brynfynnon Hotel Ferndale Shooting Club. Licensed and insured, it welcomes everyone from beginners to experienced shooters, offering a variety of targets and taster sessions, along with group and party bookings Ferndale Shooting ClubWTSF. The club is friendly and inclusive, with members always ready to help over a cup of tea Ferndale Shooting Club. It’s affiliated with CPSA, WCTSA, and WTSF, and even fields a competitive team, the Ferndale Falcons, while organizing shooting trips both locally and abroad

Name: Crynant Shooting Ground
Full Address: Crynant, NEATH SA10 8PP, South Wales
Description: Crynant Shooting Ground is a clay pigeon shooting venue set in a serene woodland area within South Wales’ Neath Valley, on the edge of the mountains. It offers a full suite of clay shooting disciplines—Sporting, Skeet, Automatic Ball Trap (ABT), Down-The-Line (DTL), and Euro‑Trap—all year round. Whether you're new to shooting or experienced, the club welcomes all levels and provides taster sessions and tuition with expert instructors, including options for juniors. Set within the Brecon National Park, the facility features a cosy clubhouse and parking for up to 50 vehicles, and it’s also open for group and corporate bookings, stag or hen parties, and team‑building events.

Name: Clay Shooting in Wales
Full Address: Near hendryd waterfalls, Coelbren, Neath SA10 9PH
Description: Clay Shooting in Wales offers a welcoming bi-weekly Sunday club that embraces shooters of all experience levels—novices, enthusiasts, and everyone in between. Sessions begin at around 10:30 am held on Fridays, Saturdays, Sundays, and Bank Holidays, with flexibility available for additional days and times upon request. The experience includes a mix of various clay traps, shaded seating, tea, coffee, snacks, and accessible facilities, including toilets and wheelchair access. They provide 1‑to‑1 lessons with BASC‑Accredited Shotgun Coaches covering safety, handling, and technique, plus “fault‑finding” help for those wanting to polish their skills. Gift vouchers are also available for experiences, lessons, and group bookings.
Practice Notes: Friday, Saturday, Sunday, plus bookings at other times

Name: Chessgrove Shooting
Full Address: Chessgrove Farm, Chessgrove, Longhope, Glos, GL17 0QD
Description: Chessgrove Shooting is a welcoming, family-run clay pigeon shooting ground set in the scenic Forest of Dean countryside. The venue features seven varied stands, each offering two trap directions, from gentle rising and crossing clays to fast, challenging targets like bolting rabbits. It caters to all levels, from complete beginners to experienced shots. All equipment is provided, including guns, cartridges, clays, and hearing protection, and every session is supervised by friendly, knowledgeable instructors. The club also offers tailored packages for stag and hen parties, birthdays, and corporate groups. For seasoned shooters, simulated game days are available, offering an immersive field-style experience with hospitality included.
